deep_dive.title: Supersede: Diagnosing and Training the Memory-Update Gap in LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents operate over long, multi-session interactions in which facts change: a user moves, a price updates, a plan is revised. Acting correctly requires using the current value of a fact and discarding values that have been superseded. We isolate this ability on real conversational data and show that it is a distinct, unsolved failure. On the knowledge-update subset of LongMemEval, replacing an agent's full context with a bounded, self-maintained memory drops accuracy from 92% to 77% even on a frontier model (gpt-5.4), a gap that is statistically significant (paired McNemar p<0.005) and persists across model scale while full-context accuracy saturates near 92%. The bottleneck is therefore memory maintenance, not comprehension, and is not closed by a stronger model. We then ask whether this is merely an undersized memory, and find it is not: as the conversation grows 24x, accuracy falls further (from 68% to 28%), and granting the agent proportionally more memory yields no detectable recovery (28% to 28%, n=25). The failure scales with the length of the conversation, not the compression ratio. We release Supersede, an open reinforcement-learning environment (on the verifiers / prime-rl stack) that turns this measurement into a training signal: agents are rewarded for answering from the current value and penalized for stale ones. Finally, we close the loop and show the gap is trainable: GRPO fine-tuning a small open model (Qwen2.5-3B) on this environment nearly doubles its held-out supersession accuracy on real, unseen conversations (9.0% to 16.7%, a single run), along a monotonic checkpoint curve indicating the learned policy, not the harness, carries the gain. To our knowledge this is the first trainable environment whose reward targets temporal fact-currency, and the first evidence the supersession gap can be trained down, not only measured.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Diagnoses memory-update gap in LLM agents and proposes training method
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 诊断并训练LLM智能体记忆更新失败问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 诊断并训练LLM智能体记忆更新失败问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 诊断并训练LLM智能体记忆更新失败问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Supersede isolates the memory-update gap in LLM agents showing a 15% accuracy drop when using bounded memory; it proposes diagnostic metrics and training strategies to improve memory maintenance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文发现LLM智能体在记忆维护环节存在显著短板：使用自维护记忆时准确率从92%降至77%。作者将这一失败归因为记忆更新缺口，并提出诊断方法和训练策略，有效缩小了该差距，为长期智能体记忆管理提供了重要改进方向。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文发现LLM智能体在记忆维护环节存在显著短板：使用自维护记忆时准确率从92%降至77%。作者将这一失败归因为记忆更新缺口，并提出诊断方法和训练策略，有效缩小了该差距，为长期智能体记忆管理提供了重要改进方向。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: memory management strategies in LLM agents

deep_dive.title: DMV-Bench: Diagnosing Long-Horizon Multimodal Agents' Visual Memory with Incidental Cue Injection
deep_dive.abstract: Research on agent memory has matured rapidly, but almost entirely on the text side: few existing benchmarks ask, in an interactive environment, when an agent genuinely needs to remember what it saw rather than what it could write down. We introduce DMV-Bench (Code: https://github.com/yyyujintang/DMV-Bench), the first interactive benchmark for multimodal-agent visual memory. DMV-Bench is built on a controlled home-furnishing e-commerce catalogue of 1,000 product variants in which a text-leakage contract keeps the discriminative signal of each task in the pixels alone. Across a chain of autonomous shopping sessions, every visited product image carries a unique, pre-rendered incidental cue, and the agent is later asked to recall a particular cued product and navigate to its URL. Inspired by dual-coding theory, we propose DualMem, a memory architecture that maintains a visual and a verbal code in parallel. On DMV-Bench, DualMem outperforms a caption baseline and three recent multimodal agent-memory systems at every chain length J in {5, 10, 15, 50} on both Gemini 2.5 Flash and Qwen2.5-VL-7B, with the lead surviving controls for memory-bank size and encoding-position bias, and an asymmetric dual-coding regime in which vision carries the cue end-to-end while the verbal channel plays a smaller query-grounding role.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: first interactive benchmark for multimodal-agent visual memory evaluation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 首个针对多模态代理视觉记忆的交互式基准评测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 首个针对多模态代理视觉记忆的交互式基准评测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 首个针对多模态代理视觉记忆的交互式基准评测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces DMV-Bench, the first interactive benchmark for multimodal-agent visual memory, using incidental cue injection to isolate visual recall from text leakage.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有代理记忆评测几乎完全基于文本的问题，提出DMV-Bench，首个交互式多模态代理视觉记忆基准。通过精心设计的商品目录环境，在每次访问产品图像时注入唯一视觉线索，并控制文本泄露。代理需在后续任务中回忆视觉细节，从而隔离评测纯视觉记忆能力。基准提供了对长时视觉记忆的诊断。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有代理记忆评测几乎完全基于文本的问题，提出DMV-Bench，首个交互式多模态代理视觉记忆基准。通过精心设计的商品目录环境，在每次访问产品图像时注入唯一视觉线索，并控制文本泄露。代理需在后续任务中回忆视觉细节，从而隔离评测纯视觉记忆能力。基准提供了对长时视觉记忆的诊断。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: How is memory evaluated in LLM agent systems?
